mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate decline below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98% as of May 2008 indicates near-zero policy rate environment
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following credit shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 0.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,754 thousand in May 2008 reflects early labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite payroll decline, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) contraction combined with housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,000 thousand units signals demand-side recession risk that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,000 thousand units AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,032 thousand in April 2008 near critical threshold
fact:RSAFS at 385,448 million in May 2008 shows demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both HOUST < 1,000 and RSAFS declines, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline below 110.0 index level signals supply-side contraction and reduced corporate capacity utilization, lowering earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.1807 in April 2008 approaching contraction threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 110.0, or Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO level and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rise above 5.5% combined with accelerating payroll decline signals labor market shock that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% AND PAYEMS declines > 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% in May 2008 at critical level
regime_hint: labor market shock phase of financial crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the joint signal provides no better predictive accuracy than UNRATE alone (AUC difference < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remaining below 215.5 index level while FEDFUNDS is cut signals real rate compression that supports equity valuations through lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing with contained inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the real rate signal (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL momentum) shows rank correlation < 0.05 with forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) sustained below 1,100 thousand units over two consecutive months signals structural demand collapse in residential investment that cascades to broader economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST remains below 1,100 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,032 thousand in April 2008 indicates housing crisis deepening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when HOUST < 1,100 for 2+ months, or Rank IC between HOUST level and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 5.5% signals stagflationary supply shock that compresses equity multiples through earnings uncertainty and higher risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflationary supply shock with rising unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO decline × UNRATE level) adds no incremental explanatory power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.03).
